item.title: Apple, Google Blast Canada’s Plan to Expand Police Data Powers
item.description: A proposed law in Canada allowing police more access to citizens’ data faces a growing chorus of criticism from technology companies, including threats that they’ll pull out of the country.

item.title: Webster Investors Approve $12 Billion Purchase by Santander
item.description: Webster Financial Corp.’s shareholders accepted the $12 billion takeover proposal from Banco Santander SA, clearing one of the last remaining hurdles for the deal.

item.title: Citadel Securities Warns That Fed Risks ‘Falling Behind Curve’
item.description: The Federal Reserve should shift closer toward hiking interest rates as rising consumer prices become the dominant threat to the economy, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: KNDS on Track For Initial Public Offering as Sales Surge
item.description: KNDS NV said it’s pushing ahead with plans to go public as the Franco-German tank maker seeks to capitalize on surging demand that drove earnings and sales last year.

item.title: Rocket, Satellite Stocks Surge as SpaceX IPO Fuels Euphoria
item.description: Space and satellite-related stocks soared Tuesday as investor enthusiasm around the industry intensified after Elon Musk’s SpaceX filed for a public offering last week.

item.title: Dudley Says Fed Credibility at Risk on Years of Missing 2% Goal
item.description: Former New York Federal Reserve President Bill Dudley warned the US central bank is in danger of losing credibility as an inflation fighter after a longstanding failure to reach its 2% goal.

item.title: Micron Shares Rally, UBS Projects $1.8 Trillion Market Value
item.description: Micron Technology Inc.’s market capitalization, which has soared about 700% over the past year, will more than double over the next 12 months, according to at least one analyst.

item.title: American Airlines to Equip 500 Jets With SpaceX Starlink Wi-Fi Starting 2027
item.description: American Airlines Group Inc. plans to install SpaceX’s Starlink satellite internet service across more than 500 narrowbody aircraft beginning in early 2027, deepening an industrywide race among carriers to offer faster and more reliable onboard connectivity as they compete for premium travelers.

item.title: Qualcomm Strikes AI Chip Deal With TikTok Owner ByteDance
item.description: Qualcomm Inc. reached a deal with TikTok owner ByteDance Ltd. to supply chips for artificial intelligence data centers, according to people familiar with the matter, marking a key win for a company trying to expand from smartphone processors into AI infrastructure.

item.title: US Consumer Confidence Slips Amid Widespread Price Worries
item.description: US consumer confidence edged down in May as views of current economic conditions settled back amid rising prices due to the war in Iran.

item.title: Musk’s xAI Warns Staffers to Limit Contact With Cursor Employees
item.description: Employees at Elon Musk’s xAI were warned by the company’s top lawyer to carefully moderate their interactions with workers from Cursor — a directive that came weeks after Musk’s firm announced a possible deal to acquire the AI coding startup.

item.title: Revel to Merge With EQT-Backed Voltera, Uniting EV Charging Networks
item.description: Private equity-backed Voltera and Revel Transit Inc. have agreed to merge their electric-vehicle charging businesses to serve ride-hail cars and robotaxis across urban areas in the US.

item.title: Altman-Backed Nuclear Firm Oklo Rises on US Reactor Fuel Program
item.description: Oklo Inc. shares jumped after the the Sam Altman-backed reactor technology company said it’s in talks to participate in a US Department of Energy program to supply nuclear fuel, an effort aimed at relieving potential bottlenecks that threaten to slow the deployment of fission power.

item.title: Warner Boosts Loan Sale Again, Plans to Repay $15 Billion Bridge
item.description: Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. boosted a loan sale for a second time, a move that will allow the media giant to fully replace $15 billion of short-term financing.

item.title: Stocks Gain on Optimism Over US-Iran Deal to Open Hormuz
item.description: Equities climbed and US oil prices were lower as investors bet that a deal to re-open the Strait of Hormuz was close, even as hostilities flared to test the fragile ceasefire.

item.title: Chile’s New Government Cries Foul Over Past Debt Projections
item.description: Chile’s new government said it’s facing a steeper fiscal challenge than even it had anticipated, claiming the previous administration of President Gabriel Boric had overestimated revenue and turned a blind eye to some expenses.

item.title: US Natural Gas Rises on Lower Output, Higher Flows to LNG Plants
item.description: US natural gas futures increased as producers reduced output over the US holiday weekend and more domestic gas supplies flowed to liquefied natural gas export terminals on the Gulf Coast. Some of those facilities had previously lowered capacity to perform seasonal maintenance, pushing more gas into the domestic market.

item.title: First Brands Hit by $286 Million Claim for Alleged Tariffs Fraud
item.description: The US government has joined the line of creditors impacted by alleged fraud at bankrupt auto-parts maker First Brands, in this instance for accusations the company cheated on how much it should have paid on tariffs.

item.title: Irish Government Agrees Bill to Ban Israeli Settlement Trade
item.description: Ireland’s cabinet agreed to put a bill to parliament that proposes a ban on the trade of goods with Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territory.

item.title: Google’s Fitbit Air Gives Whoop Some Serious Competition
item.description: The Fitbit Air, a new $100 screenless wearable from Alphabet Inc.’s Google, represents a major evolution in what consumers can expect from fitness trackers as tech companies race into an era of personalized health and artificial intelligence-powered wellness insights.

item.title: ECB to Do ‘Everything in Its Power’ to Tame Inflation: Sleijpen
item.description: The European Central Bank will do whatever is needed to bring consumer-price growth back to target, with officials to have much more information at June’s meeting as they formulate their plans, Governing Council member Olaf Sleijpen said.

item.title: Insurance Underwriter Safepoint Seeks $283.3 Million in US IPO
item.description: Safepoint Holdings Inc., an underwriter of specialty homeowners and commercial insurance, is seeking to raise as much as $283.3 million in an US initial public offering.

item.title: Bessent Has Limited Options to Halt Climb in Treasury Yields
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is facing what may become his biggest financial-market test yet, in the form of a steady increase in benchmark Treasury yields that’s generating economic headwinds and leaving him with few easy options.

item.title: Goldman Analysts Say UK Homebuilder Selloff Has Gone Too Far
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. says the selloff in UK homebuilder stocks has been excessive and investors are too pessimistic on the sector’s long-term profitability.

item.title: Kenya Plans $772 Million Green Bonds to Boost Agriculture Output
item.description: Kenya plans 100 billion shillings ($772 million) of green bonds to boost agricultural production, climate resilience and competitiveness.

item.title: PGIM Backs $4 Billion of US Land-Bank Deals in Asset-Based Push
item.description: Prudential Financial Inc.’s asset-management arm has financed about $4 billion of land-banking projects through a partnership with Domain Real Estate Partners, part of a push to gain exposure to the US homebuilding industry.

item.title: Wall Street’s AI Winner Hunt Leads to Seasoning Maker in Japan
item.description: The beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence buildout are spreading far beyond technology high-flyers. Laura Lau found one in seasoning maker Ajinomoto Co.

item.title: Hungary Holds Its Key Interest Rate With June Cut Seen in Play
item.description: Hungary’s central bank kept its key interest rate unchanged at the second-highest level in the European Union, before potentially weighing monetary easing next month.

item.title: Crypto Exchange OKX Makes AI Skills a Part of Worker Evaluations
item.description: Crypto exchange OKX is taking a page from the playbook of the tech and finance industries by linking employee evaluations to proficiency in using artificial intelligence.

item.title: Russia’s Key Black Sea Port Loads Crude After Ukraine’s Attack
item.description: Russia’s key Black Sea oil export terminal, targeted by Ukrainian drones over the weekend, is loading crude, according to satellite data.
